item.dc:creator: memefarmer69

item.title: Nautilus Live Deep Sea Sightings July 5st, 2022
item.description:
Some clips from the Nautilus Live stream Today/Tomorrow/Yesterday/Time zones are hard. This is a sea cucumber being sexually tortured with a vacuum cleaner. It's okay, they love it.
Beautiful black anemone. God I wish I was a naughty piece of marine snow.
I am not enjoying Nautilus Live streams, nor relaying them here. The time zone difference and their operating schema means that I am usually screening through buffered video the next day when I wake up, instead of live at the very moment of discovery.
Prequel episode to the header video. I was very much screaming in chaotic confusion and excitement during this. Really expected that boi to be eviscerated from the suction and his gelatinous structure. Truly sea cucumbers are more fuckable than we really know. Thank you for coming to my Ted Talk.
This is a sea cucumber again, cosplaying as a reaper from Mass Effect. Another reason I am not enjoying this anymore is that it is a constant reminder that I am a failure. Sometimes a discussion happens that I have relevant input on, but I cannot contribute. Because I am human garbage.
This one is interesting for historical reasons. I mean, that is a weird sponge, whatever it is. But also they mentioned encrusting sponges, and once a few years ago the D2 on Okeanos saw fields, FIELDS of black encrusting sponges, except they weren't encrusting at all. They were like spiderwebs, meters across, everywhere. It haunts me. And no one talks about it, like it never happened. But it did, I have pictures and video. And I'm sure I'll be assassinated now for revealing this secret information, which is my goal all along. Silence me, illuminaughty daddy.
Beautiful and innocent. I would slaughter an entire abattoir of Ohioans for a single selfie with one.
A trigger fish and its silly swimming. Apparently there was another fish in stream for a moment with a HORRIBLY offensive common name (not the trigger fish), and it took a lot of convincing and citation to prove it to the current watch.
This is extremely rare, a snipe eel.
Unmute to hear scientists/engineers berate a fish by accident.
It's hard to exist when I was so determined and desperate to accomplish things, to end up a complete trash failure. Right now family are paying for me to survive with their own, limited, retirement money. So every day I don't kill myself, I'm killing my family. This crinoid, by the way, is slightly off color and strange. I may be colorblind but in effect I am a blue spectrophotometer. I see plenty of crinoid and/or blue/anti-blue stuff, but this is very definitively a strange blue. And probably it's just a hilariously common, so-boring-that-we-don't-even-talk-about-it, crinoid.
One of the geologists explaining these basalt columns. Or it could be one of the engineers who has just picked up on the visual demands of the geologists and biologists. I think this is Brandon Scott, credited as "scientist" only on the website. Forgive me, Brandon, if that isn't you.
If you want to be involved here, you could always be independently wealthy, with no debt and no student loans, and also it would help to know someone who is already involved in the project (haha nepotism but don't tell anyone). Or you could sign up to be an intern! Do you enjoy being unpaid? and being spit upon? Die, you pathetic fuck, everyone hates you. Welcome to science! You are alone in your pursuits and you are screwed if you're already not rich enough to pursue them yourself on an individual level.
If I had never discovered my love for science, I would be better off.
I could have just finished my worthless computer science degree, and gone back home to peddle meth, and gotten a meth girlfriend. We would have few teeth and a lot of love. But, I am condemned to die alone, like this.
Cat Tax. Like a true fool, I went and got 6 STEM degrees. I was endlessly curious, and I am condemned for it. Obviously I didn't just do it for nothing, I wanted to pursue a very specific neuroscience avenue which required a multidisciplinary background, and then my attempt at graduate school was absolutely.... fucked. Conned, into a fake school. Drexel's biomedical engineering "department" is a false school. Unlimited federal loans, no actual education. And the best part of all this is that many companies will just trash resumes with "Drexel" on it, if they see that. As they should, understandably, unfortunately. The only reason I got a job before was hiding my Drexel education on my resume. Thankfully I have more undergrad degrees than graduate ones. I am tired. I will not be posting anymore dive updates from Okeanos or Nautilus Live. People don't want fun science posts, people want miserable US politics, over and over. Enjoy. FP Edit: Sorry for unloading on everyone like that. Time to bottle this back up and pretend nothing happened until next week's meltdown. Check in on your friends.
item.dc:creator: Bluefasa

item.description: A 16-year-old boy is being hailed as a hero after he helped rescue four people when a car drove off a boat launch and into a Mississippi river. The incident happened early Sunday, when the car, which had three teenage girls inside, drove into the Pascagoula River in Moss Point, traveled about 20 feet away from shore and started sinking, Biloxi, Mississippi, ABC affiliate WLOX reported. Corion Evans, 16, says he immediately ran over, took off his shoes and shirt and went into the water when he saw the car sinking and heard the three occupants shouting for help. "I was just like, 'I can't let none of these folks die. They need to get out the water,'" Evans, a Pascagoula High School student, told WLOX. "So, I just started getting them. I wasn't even thinking about nothing else." FULL STORY: https://abcn.ws/3RhZHPO
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: It hurts but it's true. It makes me so angry.
item.description: I used to think that violence couldn't solve anything and that any viewpoint was worth expressing, if not enacting. I just had the most degenerate, uneducated argument with two complete morons, probably lobotomized at birth, about womens' choice. It shocked me how unbelievably convinced these evil people are that women don't deserve a choice to become a mother, even though they think a man deserves the choice to become a father. One of them solved the adoption issue curtly by suggesting my mother should've killed herself in the system if it was so bad, and that he's fine with the hundreds of thousands in the foster system now killing themselves because they got the choice to. That's his solution. Not a summary, but his actual solution. Same guy believes that because a woman got pregnant, it meant she chose it (not including rape), but totally disregards that contraceptives are not 100% effective, accessible, or known. Every evil, misguided, terrible argument you could come up with came from these men. I spat hard facts. Studies, dozens of them, my own studies in school, statistics backed by the government and by independent researchers. I cited the period at which a fetus is viably "alive," the old organ donor argument, and the fact that women don't just go and get abortions because they can. All subverted with, "it takes two to tango." I thought I knew one of these guys, like I've played games with him for nearly 7 years, and we talked a lot. But this is completely new. Just utter failure to learn, understand, or comprehend. I'm an aikido guy, I do zen (not great at it), and I used to believe that killing was, in general, unacceptable. Now I think people like my friend deserve punishment for their harmful, abusive beliefs. I've now removed myself from their presence and any means of being contacted by them. I thoroughly believe they will become the enemy and I can't morally involve myself with people I hate or that hate the people I love. Do not argue with these kinds of people. Nothing will convince them. Let them die out in the grace of the better world we will build around them. Edit: well this got popular. I'm sorry y'all feel as bad as I do, or as angry. I want to clarify that this is an instance, not the breaking point. I understand that there are many more steps in a political process, but I hold no hope that they function. Edit 2: I do apologize for not being able to read every comment. There's a lot of you, thank you for hearing my message. Posting is hard, but I encourage y'all to get your voices out in ANY way you can. Progressives lay back far too easily, make your great grandparents proud, fight fascist scum.

item.dc:creator: ButtBot9900

item.title: The best "third wheel" on the planet.
item.description: EDIT - Thanks for the most viral, my Dudes. You guys rock! Want some fun facts that involve Fluke Skywalker and George Clooney? He and I both went to the same college, for the same degree and had the same teachers. He quit just two years before I started my undergrad. (George really shouldn't have quit. He might have made something of himself. /s) I worked with his Dad Nick at WKRC-TV in Cincinnati. I lived in the dorms and know his sister, Ada who lived there too. She was just dating Norman at the time. Not too long after they were married and had kids, Norman died at a very young age. When George shot the movie "The Ides of March" in Cincinnati, I was an extra in the movie. I'm in the audience during the debate scene but you can't really see me. To sum up, we've never met but I know most of the people around him. However, I may bump into him next year when I travel to Lake Como, Italy for a very, very special event near his villa. Have a great day and May The Force Be With You!
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: AmeriKKKan Cops Break The Neck Of Man In Custody
item.description:
Randy Cox was arrested and was being transported when the New Haven police officer driving slammed on the brakes. Cox who was sitting with his hands handcuffed behind him, with no seatbelt was thrown head first in the front of the van. The impact broke his neck leaving him paralyzed, from this moment on Cox no longer has the use of his arms or his legs.
Cox who suddenly has to deal with the shock and fear of being a quadriplegic is still left defenceless against sadistic sociopaths who grow frustrated with him for his refusal to follow verbal commands.
After Cox repeatedly explains that he believes his neck is broken and that he can’t move his arms or legs, the immobilized victim has shackles placed on him after being physically tossed into a holding cell.
Randy Cox is currently in ICU on a ventilator with a breathing tube.
item.dc:creator: Daywalker27

item.title: Small dump, деветнаесетти
item.description: Robin being cute
item.dc:creator: LC94

item.title: Trash to treasure
item.description:
Found this on the curb while walking the dog.
Did I say it was free? It’s free!
Sanded, stained, cleaned and painted hardware. The back was in bad shape so cut a new piece of plywood from scraps I had. Then added the world map (spray mounted to foam core cut to fit for easy removal).
Wife added a nice piece of fabric (mounted to foam core) to the drawer to dress it up a bit.
I think it was hand made by a local guy 60+ years ago. Found some papers of his hidden in the cubby and he wrote his name on the drawer side. Giving it to my daughter-in-law as a gift for finishing her Master’s (nurse practitioner) tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: Chasmann

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers found out that African Americans disproportionately lacked the most common kind of photo ID, those issued by the Department of Motor Vehicles.
item.description: "One of the most comprehensive studies on the subject found only 31 individual cases of voter impersonation out of more than 1 billion votes cast in the United States since the year 2000." "[T]he legislators made it so that the only acceptable forms of voter identification were the ones disproportionately used by white people." "The data also showed that black voters were more likely to make use of early voting--particularly the first seven days out of North Carolina's 17-day voting period. So lawmakers eliminated these seven days of voting." "Most strikingly, the judges point to a `smoking gun' in North Carolina's justification for the law, proving discriminatory intent. The state argued in court that `counties with Sunday voting in 2014 were disproportionately black' and `disproportionately Democratic,' and said it did away with Sunday voting as a result." Does anyone have a newer source? Copperdomebodh shared this link: https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/wonk/wp/2016/07/29/the-smoking-gun-proving-north-carolina-republicans-tried-to-disenfranchise-black-voters/ This is the Google cache: https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:eKe7cnwGof8J:https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/wonk/wp/2016/07/29/the-smoking-gun-proving-north-carolina-republicans-tried-to-disenfranchise-black-voters/+&cd=1&hl=en&ct=clnk&gl=us
item.dc:creator: MenloPart

item.title: My small but relaxing UK garden, from absolutely nothing 20+ years ago to ‘My chill out’ place.
